title: Elegant French Cheese Souffle
description: A light, fluffy French cheese souffle with umami depth, a subtle hint of chili, finished with a sweet and tangy honey lemon glaze.
instructions: Preheat oven to 190°C (375°F) and butter four 200ml ramekins, sprinkling each with a pinch of flour to prevent sticking.

In a saucepan, melt 30g butter over medium heat, add flour, and whisk for 1 minute to form a roux, then gradually whisk in milk until smooth; stir in grated cheese, chili powder, salt, pepper, and lemon zest until melted and silky.
Remove the cheese sauce from heat and let it cool slightly, then whisk in egg yolks one by one, mixing until fully incorporated.
In a separate bowl, beat egg whites with a pinch of salt until soft peaks form, then gradually add 1 tsp sugar (optional) and continue until stiff peaks appear.
Gently fold the beaten whites into the cheese mixture, preserving as much aeration as possible, then spoon into the prepared ramekins, filling each about 3/4 full.
Place the ramekins on a baking sheet and bake for 18 minutes, or until the souffles puff up and the tops are golden brown.
While baking, prepare the glaze by whisking together honey, lemon juice, and a tablespoon of melted butter until glossy.
Remove the souffles from the oven, drizzle the glaze over each, and serve immediately to maintain fluffiness.
Garnish with chopped chives if desired, and enjoy the umami, light, and slightly spicy elegance of the dish.
